domain: dynamical systems theory (Hamiltonian mechanics / KAM theorem)
specific_mechanism: Kolmogorov-Arnold-Moser theorem: in a Hamiltonian system, most invariant quasi-periodic tori survive a small enough perturbation. The surviving tori are those whose frequencies satisfy a Diophantine condition (sufficiently irrational). Tori with near-rational frequencies (resonant) are destroyed; the phase space gets a fractal structure of surviving tori interlaced with chaotic resonance zones.
llm_application: Predict which capabilities of a deployed LLM will survive a fine-tune perturbation using a KAM-style Diophantine separation measure on capability representations. Most capabilities survive small fine-tunes; the fragile ones are those whose representations are 'close' (rational-ratio analog) to the fine-tune signal's representation. Build a pre-fine-tune diagnostic that flags fragile capabilities by representational-neighborhood density.
novelty_claim: Safety basin literature (arxiv 2405.17374 Navigating Safety Landscape; 2506.17209 Fine-Tuning Lowers Safety) empirically observes that random perturbations within a 'basin' maintain capability but sharp drops occur outside. Safety-Aware Probing Optimization (2505.16737) does pre-fine-tune diagnostic. Massive Supervised Fine-tuning study (2506.14681) characterizes how data/layer/training factors shape alignment. The KAM Diophantine-separation framing of capability fragility under fine-tune perturbation is not directly published.
